title: SharePoint Online Bulk File Download Anomaly (Short Window)
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload=SharePoint Operation="FileDownloaded" earliest=-1h
| eval actor=coalesce(UserId, UserPrincipalName, userId, "")
| stats count by actor
| eventstats perc95(count) as p95
| eval threshold=max(200, ceil(5*p95))
| where count > threshold
| sort -count
description: A user downloading far more SharePoint files than peers in a one-hour window may indicate mass exfiltration, compromised credentials, or misconfigured sync clients.
value: Provides a statistical alternative to static thresholds so SOCs catch insiders and attackers without tuning per-department baselines manually.
implementation: Adjust the minimum floor (`200`) to tenant size. Exclude known migration or eDiscovery service principals via lookups. For very large tenants, run on summary data or increase window to 4h. Validate `Operation` remains `FileDownloaded` after Microsoft schema updates.
visualization: Table (actor × download count), timechart of total downloads, single value (flagged actors).
